title: 19-Year-Old Kimi Antonelli Makes F1 History with Third Straight Win in Miami
summary: Imagine being 19 years old and already breaking records in the fastest cars on the planet. That's what Kimi Antonelli just did at the Miami Grand Prix. The Italian Mercedes driver won his third Formula 1 race in a row—all from the very front starting spot, called pole position. No one in F1 history has ever done that before! Antonelli beat last year's world champion, Lando Norris, by 3.2 seconds at the Hard Rock Stadium track. The race was full of drama. Norris actually led for a while, but Antonelli's team made a clever pit stop call. They brought him in for fresh tires one lap before Norris, and that tiny edge was enough to snatch the lead. "How did we not win this? We should have won, guys," Norris said on the radio after the race. Meanwhile, Max Verstappen, a four-time champion, spun his car completely around on the first lap but somehow didn't hit anything! He dropped from the front row to 10th place but fought back to finish fifth. Charles Leclerc had a rough day too—he led early but crashed into a barrier and limped home sixth. Antonelli now has 100 points in the championship, 20 more than his teammate George Russell. "This is just the beginning, the road is still long," he said. With four races done and all won by Mercedes, the young star is already making people wonder: could he become the next legend of F1?
why_it_matters: This proves that even teenagers can become global sports heroes through talent and teamwork, reminding kids that big dreams don't have to wait until you're an adult.

keywords.explanation: Pole position is the very first starting spot in a race, which the fastest qualifier gets.
A pit stop is when a race car pulls into the team area to get new tires or repairs, costing precious seconds.
A world champion is the driver who won the most points in a whole Formula 1 season and holds the title.
When a car spins, it twirls around out of control, often losing speed and positions.
Barriers are the strong walls along a racetrack that protect drivers and fans if a car crashes.
A legend in sports is someone so amazing and successful that people remember them for a very long time.

questions.question: Who won the Miami Grand Prix?
How many races in a row has Kimi Antonelli now won?
What does 'pole position' mean?
options: The middle of the starting grid
The last place on the track
The best pit crew in the race

questions.question: What happened to Max Verstappen on the first lap?
options: He spun his car completely around but didn't hit anything
He took the lead and never lost it
He crashed into a barrier and had to quit
His tire flew off and rolled away
questions.correct_answer: He spun his car completely around but didn't hit anything

questions.question: Which team does Kimi Antonelli drive for?
How many points does Antonelli now have in the championship?

background_read: Formula 1 is the world's top racing series, with Grands Prix held every year in glamorous cities like Miami, Monaco, and Singapore.
Kimi Antonelli (an Italian driver for Mercedes) is only 19, making his record even more stunning—very few drivers win a race in their first season, let alone three in a row.
Lando Norris (the 2024 world champion) and Max Verstappen (a four-time champion from the Netherlands) are much more experienced, but Antonelli beat them with smart team strategy and quick thinking.
Article_Structure: WHO: Kimi Antonelli, the Mercedes Formula 1 driver
WHAT: He won his third straight F1 race from pole position at the Miami Grand Prix, a historic first.
WHERE: On the racetrack built around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida.
WHY: A clever pit stop call and his raw speed gave him the edge over older, more famous champions.

perspectives.description: Fans are thrilled to see a young, fresh face shake up a sport often ruled by seasoned veterans. Antonelli’s wins bring a burst of energy and hope for the future.
Some worry that a 19-year-old rising so quickly faces huge stress and that a dangerous crash could happen if he pushes too hard. The mental toll could be heavy.
Other teams and drivers are studying how Mercedes used pit stops to outsmart the competition, showing that racing success is as much about brains in the garage as it is about talent on the track.
